political.s: No specific PAC or direct political contributions from Noodles & Company were found in available federal records. As a restaurant industry member, the company is part of broader industry lobbying efforts focused on food safety, worker regulations, and tax policy.

charity.s: Noodles & Company operates a dedicated 501(c)(3) foundation providing emergency grants and educational scholarships to employees. The company has donated over $2.2 million to No Kid Hungry since 2016 and hosts hundreds of community fundraisers annually supporting local nonprofits.
charity.amt: Over $1.27 million distributed (as of 2021); ongoing fundraising partnerships
charity.focus: Employee support (emergency hardship grants, scholarships), childhood hunger alleviation, community nonprofits

environment.s: Noodles has taken steps toward sustainability including transitioning to paper straws, implementing water-efficient equipment, and using LED lighting in new locations. However, Sustainalytics rates the company's management of ESG material risk as weak, indicating room for improvement in environmental governance.

labor.s: Employees report average hourly wages of $13-14, with cashiers earning ~$14/hour and managers around $16-31/hour. Company benefits ratings are in the bottom 30% of similar companies, and employee satisfaction with compensation is low (2.9/5 stars). However, the company invests in employee benefits including childcare support and mental health resources.

dei.s: Noodles & Company has implemented pro-DEI initiatives including an Inclusion & Diversity Advisory Council, gender-neutral restrooms, employee resource groups (THRIVE and PROUD), and unconscious bias training. The company was recognized by Forbes as one of America's Best Employers for Diversity in 2022 and has increased BIPOC representation at the manager level.

animals.s: Not applicable - Noodles & Company is a restaurant chain that does not conduct animal testing. The company sources seafood responsibly through suppliers with sustainability programs.

guns.s: Not applicable - Noodles & Company is a restaurant chain and does not sell or manufacture firearms.

privacy.s: Limited public information available on Noodles & Company's comprehensive data privacy practices. The company collects customer data through its rewards app and online ordering systems but detailed privacy policies and third-party privacy assessments are not readily available.

execPay.s: CEO Joseph Christina earns $550,000 base salary with 100% target bonus. Average employee compensation is approximately $40,000-60,000 annually, suggesting a CEO-to-worker pay ratio of roughly 9-14:1. Executive compensation ratings rank in the bottom 15% compared to similar companies.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
